howItWorks.title: Four Steps to Victory
howItWorks.subtitle: From idea to award — a simple path for every student
howItWorks.step1Desc: Enter your phone number and receive a code via SMS. Registration takes 30 seconds.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Tell us about your water conservation project: video, research, art, or invention.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Other participants and viewers vote for the best projects. Get the most votes!
howItWorks.step4Desc: A jury of water resources experts evaluates the best projects. Prizes and certificates!
howItWorks.caption: HOW IT WORKS

whyWater.description: Our country ranks last in Central Asia in terms of water resources per capita. Every student project is a step toward solving this problem.

whyWater.fact2Label: of water is lost due to outdated irrigation infrastructure

testimonials.title: What participants say
testimonials.subtitle: Real stories from students and teachers
testimonials.t1Text: Thanks to Tamshy, my rainwater purification project was seen by thousands of people. I never thought a schoolgirl could influence water policy!
testimonials.t2Text: Our team made a documentary about the problems of the Caspian Sea. We received 200+ votes and an invitation to an ecology conference.
testimonials.t3Text: Tamshy inspired my students. Three of them created a model drip irrigation system for the school garden. The project works!
testimonials.t4Text: I developed an app to monitor water consumption at school. I became the contest winner and received a grant to develop the project.
testimonials.caption: REVIEWS

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most popular questions about the competition
faq.q1: Who can participate in the competition?
faq.a1: Students in grades 1-11 from any region of Kazakhstan. You can participate individually or in a team of up to 3 people.
faq.a2: Yes, participation is completely free. Registration, project upload, and voting — all at no cost.
faq.q3: What types of projects are accepted?
faq.a3: Videos, research papers, art/posters, inventions, and mobile applications. The main requirement is the topic of water conservation.
faq.a4: After moderation, the project becomes available for voting. Any registered user can vote once for each project.
faq.a5: Winners receive certificates, valuable prizes, grants for project development, and invitations to environmental conferences.
faq.a6: A research supervisor is not required, but you can specify a mentor teacher when uploading your project.
faq.a7: Yes, one participant can upload up to 3 projects in different categories.
faq.q8: What is the deadline for project submissions?
faq.a8: Project submissions are open until May 30, 2026. After this date, final voting and jury evaluation will begin.
faq.caption: QUESTIONS

about.title: About the Project
about.subtitle: Information about the Tamshy platform, its goals, objectives, and key project documents.
about.questionsDesc: Contact us and we will answer all your questions about the project.
about.goalsDesc: The Tamshy.kz platform was created to promote responsible water resource management among students of Kazakhstan. The project unites educational and competitive initiatives aimed at engaging youth in solving water-related environmental problems.
about.feature1Desc: Raising awareness among students and teachers about water resource issues in Kazakhstan.
about.feature2Desc: A platform for sharing videos, research, art projects, and inventions.
about.feature3Desc: Organizing national water project competitions for students.
about.feature4Desc: Convenient tools for project management, evaluation, and voting.
about.descP1: The Tamshy initiative is implemented with the support of the Ministry of Water Resources and Irrigation of the Republic of Kazakhstan and is aimed at environmental education of youth in the context of the Aral Sea basin problems.
about.descP2: The program covers all regions of the country and offers students the opportunity to present their own water conservation projects in the format of videos, scientific research, artwork, and technical inventions. The best works are evaluated by an expert jury of water resources specialists, and winners receive diplomas and valuable prizes.
